summary: Iteration 16 — Verified 3 NEW additions: (1) chord_tutorial settings field, (2) ChordTutorial overlay + mini keyboard, (3) note resize handle in edit mode. Backend: 18/18 pytest pass (added test_settings_chord_tutorial_default_and_persist). Frontend: chord tutorial overlay renders with correct chord name (C) and mini keyboard highlights C4/E4/G4 (chord tones) exactly as expected. Settings toggle chord-tutorial-switch present, default ON, toggling OFF hides overlay instantly. Note resize handle appears at top of selected note (data-testid=resize-handle-0), drag up 40-60px increases note height proportionally (65->105 or 71->131), saves via PUT /api/songs/{id} for user songs, and persists across page reload (verified: after reload+edit, edit-note-0 height=105.5 matches pre-reload). Chord parser verified via Node inline test: Cmaj->[60,64,67], Am->[57,60,64], G7->[55,59,62,65], Csus4->[60,65,67], C/E->[52,60,64,67], empty/X/?/undefined -> null (graceful). Video sync/regression items not exercised in this iteration (main flows validated in prior iterations).

integration_issues.flow: Edit mode save on DEMO songs
integration_issues.issue: Clicking SAVE while editing a demo song (e.g., 'Twinkle Twinkle Little Star') calls PUT /api/songs/{demo-id} which returns 404 because demo songs are baked-in and not persisted in DB. Toast: 'Could not save edits'. This is a pre-existing limitation, not new to iteration 16 (the resize gesture itself works; the underlying song.notes[0].duration was updated in-memory and the note visually grew). User songs (uploaded/refined) save & persist correctly.

passed: GET/PUT /api/settings — chord_tutorial:bool present, default=true, PUT {chord_tutorial:false} persists, restore to true works

All 17 previous regression tests still green (demo-songs, songs CRUD, refine-midi single/multi-track/cache-key/fallback, sheet-to-midi empty/corrupt/real-PDF/PNG, video ai-enhance, settings convert_mode)
Chord Tutorial overlay renders (data-testid=chord-tutorial-overlay) with chord name 'C' displayed via data-testid=chord-tutorial-name

Mini keyboard (data-testid=chord-tutorial-mini-keyboard) has 15 white-key elements; for chord 'C' the highlighted keys are exactly chord-tutorial-key-C4, chord-tutorial-key-E4, chord-tutorial-key-G4 (MIDI 60,64,67)
Settings > chord-tutorial-switch present, default aria-checked=true; toggling OFF causes chord-tutorial-overlay count to drop to 0 immediately

Note resize: resize-handle-0 appears at top of selected note (12px height, only when width >=12px); dragging up 40px increased note height 65->105 (user song) and 60px drag increased 71->131 (demo song) — proportional to drag delta
Note resize persistence on user songs: SAVE calls PUT /api/songs/{id} 200, reload page + reopen + edit shows edit-note-0 height=105.5 matches pre-reload
Chord parser correctness (Node inline): Cmaj=[60,64,67], Am=[57,60,64], G7=[55,59,62,65], Csus4=[60,65,67], Cmaj7=[60,64,67,71], C/E=[52,60,64,67], empty/X/?/undefined return null (graceful)
action_items: Optional UX: When user enters edit mode on a demo song and clicks SAVE, either (a) auto-copy the demo song as a new user song (POST /api/songs) so edits persist, or (b) disable the SAVE button + show tooltip 'Demo songs cannot be edited. Save as new song instead.' Currently the toast just says 'Could not save edits' after 404.
critical_code_review_comments: MidiEditor.jsx resize gesture uses window mousemove/touchmove listeners with startY captured on mousedown. Delta computed via (startY - clientY) / containerH * lookahead. This works but ignores rAF for smoothness; consider requestAnimationFrame batching if resize feels jittery on low-end devices.

ChordTutorial.jsx uses lastNameRef to avoid setState churn when chord name is unchanged — good perf pattern. However the effect deps include `active` which will trigger re-runs after setActive. Not causing infinite loops (guarded by nextName===lastNameRef.current) but a comment would help.

chordParser.js QUALITIES longest-match uses `>=` which for empty-string key '' (length 0) will always match first. Effectively works because '' is iterated first and any longer key overwrites it, but a stricter `>` would be safer. Not a bug now.
server.py PUT /api/songs/{song_id} returns 404 for demo songs. Consider returning a distinct 409/422 with a friendlier error body so the frontend can toast 'Demo songs are read-only'.
seed_data_creation: Created and later deleted user song 'TEST_chord_tutorial_song' with chords [{C@0s},{Am@3s},{F@6s},{G7@9s}] and 11 notes to exercise chord-tutorial overlay. Cleanup verified via DELETE /api/songs/{id} 200.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 16 new features fully verified. If future iterations need chord-tutorial testing, POST a user song with a `chords` array (each item: {time, name, duration}) then load it and press play. Chord parser rootMidi is clamped to [55,66], so higher-root chords may render an octave below their nominal position (e.g., G7 renders as G3/B3/D4/F4 — this is intentional per the code).
rca of the issue: Demo-song edit-save 404 is not a new bug; it stems from PUT /api/songs/{id} requiring a DB record. Root cause: demo songs are hardcoded server-side (or in a static list), not inserted into the songs collection, so update_one().matched_count==0 → HTTPException(404).
